news.title:
NSE selects 20 banks, including Morgan Stanley and Kotak Mahindra, for its IPO set to raise $1.5-$2.5 billion.
NSE appoints 8 law firms and completes intermediary selection; IPO committee chaired by Srinivas Injeti approves plans.
news.summary:
NSE selects 20 banks, including Morgan Stanley and Kotak Mahindra, for its IPO set to raise $1.5-$2.5 billion.
NSE appoints 8 law firms and completes intermediary selection; IPO committee chaired by Srinivas Injeti approves plans.

news.title:
Several major stocks, including Kotak Bank, HUL, and Varun Beverages, hit 52-week lows amid extended losing streaks.
Notable corrections include Five Star Finance (-57%), Tata Motors PV (-55%), and Trent (-43%) from their respective 52-week highs.
news.summary:
Several major stocks, including Kotak Bank, HUL, and Varun Beverages, hit 52-week lows amid extended losing streaks.
Notable corrections include Five Star Finance (-57%), Tata Motors PV (-55%), and Trent (-43%) from their respective 52-week highs.

news.title:
Kotak Mahindra Bank executed block trade of ~1,999,881 shares at Rs. 384.6.
Trade valued at Rs. 76.92 cr occurred on NSE.
news.summary:
Kotak Mahindra Bank executed block trade of ~1,999,881 shares at Rs. 384.6.
Trade valued at Rs. 76.92 cr occurred on NSE.

news.title:
Kotak Mahindra Bank plans acquisitions to utilize excess capital.
CEO Ashok Vaswani says M&A targets will align with strategic goals.
news.summary:
Kotak Mahindra Bank plans acquisitions to utilize excess capital.
CEO Ashok Vaswani says M&A targets will align with strategic goals.

news.title:
RBI approves Anup Saha as whole-time director at Kotak Mahindra Bank effective immediately.
Saha's career spans over 32 years, including roles at Bajaj Finance and ICICI Bank.
news.summary:
RBI approves Anup Saha as whole-time director at Kotak Mahindra Bank effective immediately.
Saha's career spans over 32 years, including roles at Bajaj Finance and ICICI Bank.

news.title:
Kotak Mahindra Bank fell 2.6% on Mar 4, closing at ₹402, extending a 6-day losing streak.
The decline of 6.6% over six days is due to technical weakness and reduced investor activity.
news.summary:
Kotak Mahindra Bank fell 2.6% on Mar 4, closing at ₹402, extending a 6-day losing streak.
The decline of 6.6% over six days is due to technical weakness and reduced investor activity.

news.title:
Kotak Mahindra Bank revises account fees, debit card charges, and transaction limits from April 1.
Monthly cash transaction limits, third-party cash transaction fees, and ECS failure charges also updated.
news.summary:
Kotak Mahindra Bank revises account fees, debit card charges, and transaction limits from April 1.
Monthly cash transaction limits, third-party cash transaction fees, and ECS failure charges also updated.

news.title:
NPS Tier II schemes offer flexible investments with no lock-in, delivering up to 18.8% in equity, 8.6% in corporate bonds, and 9.1% in government bonds.
Top performers include Tata Pension Fund (18.8% equity), HDFC Pension Fund (8.6% corporate bonds), and LIC (9.1% government bonds).
news.summary:
NPS Tier II schemes offer flexible investments with no lock-in, delivering up to 18.8% in equity, 8.6% in corporate bonds, and 9.1% in government bonds.
Top performers include Tata Pension Fund (18.8% equity), HDFC Pension Fund (8.6% corporate bonds), and LIC (9.1% government bonds).

news.title:
GIFT Nifty signals a weak start; Nifty futures down 0.5% at 25,592.5. Sensex gained 479.95 points, closing at 83,294.66.
Top Nifty gainers: Adani Ports, Dr. Reddy's, Kotak Mahindra. IT sector fell 1.4%, PSU Bank index rose 1.3%; volatility persists.
news.summary:
GIFT Nifty signals a weak start; Nifty futures down 0.5% at 25,592.5. Sensex gained 479.95 points, closing at 83,294.66.
Top Nifty gainers: Adani Ports, Dr. Reddy's, Kotak Mahindra. IT sector fell 1.4%, PSU Bank index rose 1.3%; volatility persists.

news.title:
PSBs surpass private banks as quarterly profits reach new highs.
Only 3 of 30 listed universal banks had >1% net NPAs in Q3.
news.summary:
PSBs surpass private banks as quarterly profits reach new highs.
Only 3 of 30 listed universal banks had >1% net NPAs in Q3.

news.title:
Kotak Mahindra Bank saw a 37.1% surge in derivatives open interest on 19 Feb 2026.
Despite the spike, the stock underperformed its sector and broader indices.
news.summary:
Kotak Mahindra Bank saw a 37.1% surge in derivatives open interest on 19 Feb 2026.
Despite the spike, the stock underperformed its sector and broader indices.

news.title:
New M&A funding norms present $15B opportunity for banks with strong investment banking capabilities.
Key changes include 75% acquisition financing, 3:1 debt-equity ratio, and doubled finance cap to 20% of Tier 1 capital.
news.summary:
New M&A funding norms present $15B opportunity for banks with strong investment banking capabilities.
Key changes include 75% acquisition financing, 3:1 debt-equity ratio, and doubled finance cap to 20% of Tier 1 capital.

news.title:
Kotak Mahindra Bank appoints Ramesh Iyer as Independent Director for 4 years from Feb 17, 2026, succeeding Dr. Ashok Gulati.
Iyer, with over 40 years in Mahindra Group, has held key roles and won multiple industry awards like Ernst & Young Entrepreneur of the Year.
news.summary:
Kotak Mahindra Bank appoints Ramesh Iyer as Independent Director for 4 years from Feb 17, 2026, succeeding Dr. Ashok Gulati.
Iyer, with over 40 years in Mahindra Group, has held key roles and won multiple industry awards like Ernst & Young Entrepreneur of the Year.

news.title:
RBI's revised guidelines on acquisition financing enable Indian banks like SBI, Bank of India, and Kotak Mahindra Bank to participate in M&A, refinancing, and stake hikes with eased equity requirements.
The new norms allow unlisted firms with Rs 500 crore net worth and 3-year profitability to access financing, boosting domestic consolidation and capex in sectors like manufacturing and healthcare.
news.summary:
RBI's revised guidelines on acquisition financing enable Indian banks like SBI, Bank of India, and Kotak Mahindra Bank to participate in M&A, refinancing, and stake hikes with eased equity requirements.
The new norms allow unlisted firms with Rs 500 crore net worth and 3-year profitability to access financing, boosting domestic consolidation and capex in sectors like manufacturing and healthcare.

news.title:
Kotak Mahindra Bank executed Rs. 103.3 crore NSE block trade for 2,459,567 shares.
The trade occurred at Rs. 420 per share, reflecting significant transaction volume.
news.summary:
Kotak Mahindra Bank executed Rs. 103.3 crore NSE block trade for 2,459,567 shares.
The trade occurred at Rs. 420 per share, reflecting significant transaction volume.

news.title:
Kotak Mahindra Bank CTO Bhavnish Lathia resigns citing personal reasons.
Nilesh Chaudhari takes over as CTO; Lathia's last day was February 9.
news.summary:
Kotak Mahindra Bank CTO Bhavnish Lathia resigns citing personal reasons.
Nilesh Chaudhari takes over as CTO; Lathia's last day was February 9.

news.title:
Kotak Mahindra Bank to meet investors at Axis Capital's Advantage India Conference on Feb 12, 2026.
The meeting is disclosed under SEBI Regulation 30 for investor information and dissemination.
news.summary:
Kotak Mahindra Bank to meet investors at Axis Capital's Advantage India Conference on Feb 12, 2026.
The meeting is disclosed under SEBI Regulation 30 for investor information and dissemination.

news.title:
DICGC will adopt risk-based deposit insurance premiums from April 2026, benefiting large banks like HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, Axis Bank, and Kotak Mahindra Bank with lower operating expenses.
Premium reductions could cut operating expenses by 1.5-3% and boost earnings by 1-2% for private banks, with PSU banks potentially benefiting from higher ratings.
news.summary:
DICGC will adopt risk-based deposit insurance premiums from April 2026, benefiting large banks like HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank, Axis Bank, and Kotak Mahindra Bank with lower operating expenses.
Premium reductions could cut operating expenses by 1.5-3% and boost earnings by 1-2% for private banks, with PSU banks potentially benefiting from higher ratings.

news.title:
DIPAM receives financial bids for IDBI Bank privatisation, targeting sale of 60.72% stake worth ₹72,000 crore.
Kotak Mahindra Bank, Fairfax India Holdings emerge as contenders; announcement expected by March 2026.
news.summary:
DIPAM receives financial bids for IDBI Bank privatisation, targeting sale of 60.72% stake worth ₹72,000 crore.
Kotak Mahindra Bank, Fairfax India Holdings emerge as contenders; announcement expected by March 2026.

news.title:
Kotak Mahindra Bank saw an NSE block trade of 2,782,768 shares at Rs. 412.3.
The total trade value for this transaction amounted to Rs. 114.73 crores.
news.summary:
